instruction: Walk towards the kitchen area. Keep walking along the kitchen area towards the doorway. Turn right and exit the kitchen area walking across the small living room area and into the bedroom to you left.

Final ledger:
```
Route: start (open plan facing kitchen) → 3m along kitchen island → doorway at hall (J1)
       → right through doorway past white-sofa lounge → right branch into south wing (J2)
       → past frosted-door corridor into gray sitting room ("small living room")
       → across it, through doorway into bedroom (dark duvet, yellow throw, NEW YORK poster)
Progress: towards kitchen ✓ · along kitchen to doorway ✓ · turn right, exit kitchen ✓ ·
          across small living room ✓ · into bedroom to left ✓ (stopped ~1 m inside)
Steps used: 316/500 · tool calls: 41/80
```

The deciding inference was that the instruction's "small living room area" had to be *crossed* to reach the bedroom — only the gray sitting room with the twin bedroom doorways on its far wall fit that, so I stopped just inside that bedroom rather than the hall-adjacent master.
